title: Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani

seo.title: How to Make Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani — Easy Indian Recipe

description: Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani is a classic Indian dish renowned for its rich, creamy tomato-based gravy and tender pieces of chicken. This beloved curry perfectly balances tanginess and subtle sweetness, making it a comforting and flavorful meal. It is a true culinary jewel, often enjoyed with naan or rice.

ingredients.item: Boneless, skinless chicken thighs or breast

ingredients.notes: soaked in hot water for 15 minutes

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: In a bowl, combine the chicken cubes with plain yogurt, 1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste, turmeric powder, 1 teaspoon red chili powder, 1 teaspoon garam masala, and salt. Mix well to coat the chicken evenly. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or up to 2 hours for best flavor.

instructions.title: Cook the Chicken
instructions.text: Heat 1 tablespoon butter and 1 tablespoon vegetable oil in a large pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken pieces and cook until browned on all sides and mostly cooked through, about 5-7 minutes. Remove the chicken from the pan and set aside.

instructions.title: Prepare the Gravy Base
instructions.text: In the same pan, add 1 tablespoon of butter. Add bay leaf, cinnamon stick, and green cardamom pods. Saute for 30 seconds until fragrant. Add the finely chopped onion and cook until translucent and lightly golden, about 5-7 minutes.

instructions.title: Simmer the Tomato Gravy
instructions.text: Add the remaining ginger-garlic paste, coriander powder, cumin powder, and the remaining red chili powder to the pan. Cook for 1 minute until aromatic. Stir in the tomato puree. Add the soaked cashews (drained) and 1/2 cup of water. Bring to a simmer, then cover and cook for 10-12 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the tomatoes to soften.

instructions.title: Blend and Strain the Gravy
instructions.text: Remove the whole spices (bay leaf, cinnamon, cardamom pods) if desired, or blend them in for maximum flavor. Carefully transfer the gravy mixture to a blender and blend until completely smooth. For an extra velvety texture, strain the blended gravy through a fine-mesh sieve back into the clean pan.

instructions.title: Finish the Butter Chicken
instructions.text: Return the pan with the smooth gravy to low heat. Stir in the cooked chicken, remaining 2 tablespoons of butter, heavy cream, honey or sugar, and crushed kasoori methi. Simmer gently for another 5-7 minutes, allowing the chicken to absorb the rich flavors and the sauce to thicken slightly. Adjust salt and sweetness to taste.

instructions.title: Serve Hot
instructions.text: Garnish with fresh coriander leaves if desired. Serve the authentic Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani hot with warm naan bread, roti, or steamed basmati rice for a truly delightful Indian lunch.

tips: For the smoothest gravy, ensure you blend the tomato-cashew mixture thoroughly and then pass it through a fine-mesh sieve. This removes any fibrous bits and whole spice remnants.
Always toast your kasoori methi (dried fenugreek leaves) lightly in a dry pan for a few seconds before crushing and adding to the curry. This enhances its unique aromatic flavor significantly.
To achieve tender chicken, do not overcook it in the initial step. It will finish cooking in the gravy. Marinating the chicken in yogurt helps tenderize it further and adds depth of flavor.
article:
Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani: Your Ultimate Guide to a Classic Indian Lunch Recipe

The world of Indian cuisine is a vibrant tapestry of flavors, aromas, and textures, and among its most beloved masterpieces is the iconic Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani. This dish isn't just food; it's an experience – a rich, creamy, and subtly spiced delight that has captured hearts globally. Perfect as a comforting Indian lunch recipe, Butter Chicken offers a harmonious blend of tangy tomatoes, luscious cream, fragrant spices, and tender chicken, making it an irresistible choice for any meal. If you've ever dreamed of recreating that authentic restaurant-quality taste at home, you're in the right place. This comprehensive guide will walk you through every step to master this culinary gem.

## What is Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani?

At its heart, Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani is a classic Indian curry originating from Delhi in the 1950s, credited to the Moti Mahal restaurant. The name itself, "Murgh Makhani," literally translates to "chicken with butter" – a testament to the generous use of butter and cream that defines its luxurious texture. Unlike many fiery Indian curries, Butter Chicken is celebrated for its mild, sweet, and tangy profile, making it universally appealing, even to those sensitive to spice. The dish features succulent pieces of chicken, often marinated in yogurt and spices and then grilled or pan-fried, before being bathed in a velvety smooth tomato-based sauce. This sauce is slow-cooked with a medley of aromatic spices like garam masala, kasuri methi (dried fenugreek leaves), and a touch of sweetness, finished with fresh cream for that signature richness. Its comforting nature and relatively simple flavor profile, compared to other robust curries, make it an ideal and frequently sought-after Indian lunch recipe.

## Ingredients You Need

Crafting the perfect Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani requires a thoughtful selection of fresh, high-quality ingredients. Here's what you'll need to gather:

For the Chicken Marinade:
*   500g boneless, skinless chicken (thighs or breast, cut into 1-inch pieces)
*   1/2 cup plain yogurt
*   1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste
*   1 teaspoon red chili powder (Kashmiri for color)
*   1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder
*   1 teaspoon garam masala
*   1/2 teaspoon salt
*   1 tablespoon lemon juice

For the Makhani Sauce:
*   2 tablespoons unsalted butter (plus more for cooking chicken)
*   1 large onion, finely chopped
*   1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste
*   1 (28-ounce) can crushed tomatoes (or 4-5 large fresh tomatoes, pureed)
*   1/4 cup raw cashews (soaked in warm water for 15 minutes, optional but recommended for creaminess)
*   1 teaspoon red chili powder (Kashmiri)
*   1 teaspoon ground cumin
*   1 teaspoon ground coriander
*   1/2 teaspoon garam masala
*   1/2 teaspoon sugar or honey (to balance tang)
*   1/2 teaspoon kasuri methi (dried fenugreek leaves), crushed
*   1/2 cup heavy cream (plus extra for garnish)
*   Salt to taste
*   Fresh cilantro for garnish

## Step-by-Step Instructions

Follow these steps carefully to create an exquisite Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani that will impress everyone at your lunch table.

Step 1: Marinate the Chicken
In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with all the marinade ingredients. Mix well to ensure every piece is coated. Cover the bowl and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or ideally 2-4 hours, or even overnight for maximum flavor.

Step 2: Cook the Chicken
Heat a tablespoon of butter or oil in a large pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken pieces in a single layer, making sure not to overcrowd the pan. Cook in batches if necessary. Sear the chicken for 5-7 minutes until lightly browned and cooked through, with a slight char on the edges. This char adds a wonderful smoky depth. Remove the cooked chicken from the pan and set aside.

Step 3: Prepare the Makhani Sauce Base
In the same pan, melt 2 tablespoons of butter over medium heat. Add the chopped onions and sauté until they turn soft and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the ginger-garlic paste and cook for another minute until fragrant.
Stir in the crushed tomatoes, soaked cashews (if using), Kashmiri red chili powder, ground cumin, ground coriander, and salt. Bring the mixture to a simmer, then reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for 15-20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the tomatoes have broken down and the oil begins to separate from the mixture.
Carefully transfer the cooked sauce mixture to a blender. Blend until completely smooth. For an extra silky sauce, you can pass it through a fine-mesh sieve, pressing down with a spoon.

Step 4: Combine and Finish
Pour the blended sauce back into the pan. Stir in the cooked chicken pieces. Add the garam masala, kasuri methi (crushed between your palms to release its aroma), and sugar or honey. Simmer for 5-10 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the chicken to absorb the sauce.
Finally, stir in the heavy cream. Cook for another 2-3 minutes, just until heated through – do not boil after adding cream, as it might curdle. Taste and adjust salt or sweetness if needed. Your Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani is now ready!

## Pro Tips for the Best Results

To elevate your Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani from good to absolutely phenomenal, consider these expert tips:

*   **Marination is Key:** The longer you marinate the chicken, the more tender and flavorful it will be. Overnight marination truly makes a difference.
*   **Charred Chicken:** Don't skip searing the chicken until it has a slight char. This replicates the tandoor-cooked flavor and adds an incredible depth that's crucial for authentic Butter Chicken.
*   **Silky Smooth Sauce:** Blending the sauce thoroughly is paramount. If your blender isn't powerful enough, or you want an extra-luxurious texture, strain the blended sauce through a fine-mesh sieve.
*   **Kashmiri Red Chili Powder:** This specific chili powder provides a beautiful, vibrant red color to the sauce without adding excessive heat. It's often used for its aesthetic appeal in Indian cooking.
*   **Don't Forget Kasuri Methi:** Dried fenugreek leaves (kasuri methi) contribute a unique, slightly bitter, and aromatic flavor that is quintessential to Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani. Lightly toasting it in a dry pan or crushing it between your palms before adding enhances its fragrance.
*   **Balance the Flavors:** The perfect Butter Chicken strikes a balance between sweet, tangy, and savory. Adjust the sugar/honey and salt to your preference after the sauce has simmered.

## Serving Suggestions

Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani is a versatile dish that pairs wonderfully with a variety of accompaniments, making it a complete and satisfying Indian lunch recipe.
*   **Rice:** Steamed basmati rice, fragrant jeera rice (cumin rice), or even a simple pulao are excellent choices to soak up the rich sauce.
*   **Bread:** Warm, fluffy naan bread (garlic naan or butter naan are favorites), soft rotis, or flaky parathas are perfect for scooping up every last drop of the delicious curry.
*   **Sides:** A simple fresh salad of sliced cucumbers, onions, and tomatoes, or a cooling raita (yogurt dip), can provide a refreshing contrast to the richness of the curry.
*   **Garnish:** Always finish your Butter Chicken with a swirl of fresh cream and a sprinkle of chopped fresh cilantro for presentation and an extra burst of freshness.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the classic Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani is undeniably delicious, there are several ways to adapt it to dietary preferences or availability of ingredients.
*   **Vegetarian Option:** For a meat-free version, substitute chicken with paneer (Indian cottage cheese) or firm tofu. Simply pan-fry the paneer/tofu until golden before adding it to the sauce. You can also make a Mixed Vegetable Makhani.
*   **Vegan Option:** To make it vegan, replace butter with a plant-based butter or oil, and use full-fat coconut milk or cashew cream instead of heavy cream and yogurt.
*   **Spice Level:** Adjust the amount of red chili powder to your liking. For more heat, add a finely chopped green chili along with the ginger-garlic paste. For less heat, reduce the chili powder.
*   **Healthier Twist:** Reduce the amount of butter and cream, or use low-fat yogurt in the marinade and light cream in the sauce. The cashews still provide a good amount of creaminess.
*   **Quick Version:** If you're short on time, use pre-cooked rotisserie chicken, shredded, and add it directly to the sauce to simmer. You can also use good quality store-bought tomato puree instead of cooking down fresh tomatoes. This makes Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani a fantastic meal prep option.

Mastering Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani at home is a rewarding culinary journey that brings the authentic flavors of India right to your kitchen. This rich, creamy, and subtly spiced dish, perfect as an Indian lunch recipe or a delightful dinner, is sure to become a cherished favorite. So gather your ingredients, follow these steps, and prepare to indulge in the comforting magic of homemade Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani.
